items.heading: Music Box Lesson Fuels Creative Experimentation
items.summary: RoboCamp's 'Music Box' lesson allows students to expand upon the basic version by composing music and modifying the build, promoting creative thinking.
items.text: A short lesson scenario doesn’t have to be a limitation. It can be a powerful teaching tool.  When the core of an exercise is well designed and built around a clear structure, students don’t spend the entire lesson just trying to “finish the task.” They gain time for something more important — experimentation.  In this implementation of the “Music Box” lesson from RoboCamp, students didn’t stop at the basic version. They added their own musical compositions and modified the build.  This is the moment when a lesson plan stops being an instruction sheet and becomes a starting point for creative thinking.  Great work by the team at Beyond Blocks. Congratulations!  #robocamp #LEGOEducation #SPIKEprime #STEM #STEMEducation #stemforkids #edtech

items.heading: Teaching AI with SPIKE Prime's Launcher AI+ Activity
items.summary: RoboCamp's Launcher AI+ activity uses SPIKE Prime to teach students AI learning processes without new hardware, focusing on logic design and feedback adaptation.
items.text: The recent announcement of LEGO Education’s Computer Science & AI sets raises an important question: should AI in robotics education focus on using AI tools, or on understanding how learning algorithms actually work?  Understanding how AI learns — through feedback, adaptation, and iteration — can already be taught today, even with robotics sets that many schools already have in their classrooms.  One example is Launcher AI+, an advanced SPIKE Prime activity developed by the RoboCamp team.  Students build a catapult-style robot that repeatedly launches a ball toward a target. After each attempt, the robot receives feedback — the shot was too short or too far — and adjusts its launch power accordingly. With every iteration, the program updates its parameters and gradually improves accuracy.  There is no black-box AI here. Students design the learning logic themselves and can clearly see how data and feedback shape the robot’s behavior over time.  This is what learning about AI can look like — without new hardware, and without using AI as a shortcut instead of understanding how it works.  If you’d like to learn more about this approach, you’ll find additional details in the comments below.  #robocamp #LEGOEducation #SPIKEprime #STEM #STEMEducation #stemforkids #edtech #AIinEducation #machinelearningeducation

items.heading: Continuation and Future of LEGO SPIKE in Education
items.summary: LEGO Education confirms continued support for SPIKE Prime and Essential until 2031, while introducing new Computer Science & AI sets.
items.text: LEGO® Education has announced a new Computer Science & AI product line and, at the same time, shared an update regarding the retirement of SPIKE Prime and SPIKE Essential.  For schools already working with SPIKE sets, the following confirmed timelines are particularly important: – SPIKE Prime and SPIKE Essential will remain available for purchase at least until mid-2026, – spare parts for SPIKE Prime and SPIKE Essential will be available until June 2028, – software for SPIKE will remain supported until June 2031.  This means that the well-established SPIKE Prime and SPIKE Essential sets will continue to be reliable, long-term educational tools for many years to come.  As for the new Computer Science & AI sets — at this stage, it is simply too early to evaluate them. We don’t form opinions until we have had the opportunity to work hands-on with the sets and test them in real classroom conditions.  We will publish a full review of the new sets after proper testing.  In the meantime, we invite you to read our review of LEGO Education Science. Since the new Computer Science & AI line is based on the same generation of electronic components, it is currently the best available reference point for understanding the direction LEGO Education is heading with its future educational products.  Link to the review in the comments.  #robocamp #LEGOEducation #SPIKEprime #SPIKEEssential #educationalrobotics #STEM #stemeducation #stemforkids #edtech

items.text: 💙 6 lat wspólnej pracy i ciągle nowe wyzwania! Wasz rozwój motywuje nas do tworzenia coraz lepszych scenariuszy lekcji. Bardzo cieszy nas, że do pracy z zestawami SPIKE Prime włączacie również druk 3D i ploter laserowy. Pokazujecie w praktyce, że lekcje robotyki nie kończą się na tym, co jest w pudełku – jedynym ograniczeniem jest wyobraźnia ✨  #RoboCamp #LEGOEducation #SPIKEPrime #EducationalRobotics #STEMEducation #3DPrinting #Tinkercad #3DDesign

items.heading: Cosmic Battle: Sci-Fi Coding Fun for Ages 6-9
items.summary: The Cosmic Battle lesson uses LEGO SPIKE Essential to teach students aged 6–9 coding and robotics through building and battling starfighter models.
items.text: 🚀 Cosmic Battle – a galactic duel right in your classroom! 🌌  Build two starfighters that resemble iconic ships from sci-fi universes… then let them battle it out! 🔫 Who will win? No one knows – the outcome is decided by chance! 🎲 A perfect way to introduce young students to coding and robotics through fun and unpredictability.  👧 Designed for students aged 6–9 👩‍🏫 Includes ready-to-use lesson plans, simple instructions, and Word Blocks coding 📦 Required kit: LEGO® Education SPIKE Essential (#45345)  #RoboCamp #STEMeducation #RoboticsForKids #CodingForKids #LEGOEducation #EdTech #SPIKEEssential

items.heading: 3D Printing in Robotics: Opportunity or Hindrance?
items.summary: RoboCamp explores integrating 3D printing with SPIKE Prime to enhance creative freedom in robotics lessons, discussing the balance between potential and logistical challenges.
items.text: 🚀 3D Printing for Robotics – Unlimited Potential or Unnecessary Complication?  At RoboCamp, you'll find lesson plans that incorporate 3D-printed components, specifically designed to unlock the full potential of LEGO® Education SPIKE™ Prime.  ✅ On one hand – unlimited creative freedom! Design and print exactly what you need. The only limit is your imagination!  ⚠️ On the other hand – logistical challenges. You need access to a 3D printer and must allocate time for printing, which doesn't always go smoothly.  ➡️ What do you think? Would you utilize a 3D printer in this way? Is it a fantastic method to enhance robotics classes, or is it too much trouble?  💬 Share your thoughts – your opinions are invaluable to us!

items.heading: Teaching Python with Classic Games on SPIKE Prime
items.summary: RoboCamp offers a course where students use SPIKE Prime and Python to recreate classic games like Snake and Arkanoid, enhancing programming skills and game mechanics understanding.
items.text: Snake and Arkanoid in Python? Build them yourself with SPIKE Prime! 🤖🕹️   Remember playing Snake on your first mobile phone? Or spending hours with Arkanoid on an old computer? For many of us, these games were our very first step into the digital world – first as players, then as programmers, and today as educators.  At RoboCamp, we now give kids the chance to experience something similar. Only this time — from the other side of the screen.  As part of our SPIKE Prime Programming Course Expert, students create their own versions of these classic games using LEGO kits and the Python programming language.🔧🧠💻  It’s not just fun. It’s: ✅ hands-on coding in Python, ✅ learning how game mechanics work, ✅ exploring the history of gamedev through experience.  Why do we teach kids to program Snake and Arkanoid? Because that’s how our own tech journey began. Those pixelated games sparked our curiosity and made us ask: How does it work? Could I build this myself?  Now it’s the kids’ turn. We give them the tools — and they become the creators. Python. Robots. The classics of gamedev. All ready — in our expert-level course.  Join us on this journey from pixels to robots! 💾➡️🤖  #RoboCamp #LEGOEducation #SPIKEPrime #PythonForKids #EducationalRobotics #STEMEducation

items.heading: Build a Moving Owl Model with the Flying Bird Project
items.summary: The Flying Bird project lets students build a moving owl model using SPIKE Prime or mBot 2, employing a crank mechanism and laser-cut elements.
items.text: 🦉 Flying Bird – SPIKE Prime / mBot 2 / laser cutter  Build a moving owl model with your students through the Flying Bird project. Wooden elements and string create a crank mechanism powered by a motor, illustrating mechanical principles in practice. • Construction of a working mechanism • Understanding the principle of a crank • Development of design skills  The model can be constructed using the LEGO SPIKE Prime or mBot 2 set, along with elements cut with a laser cutter, allowing for the creation of complex mechanisms.  Get this lesson plan and more: bit.ly/robocamp_offer

items.text:
🤖 8 new robots for your lessons!

items.heading: Measure Wind with a 3D Printed Anemometer
items.summary: Students build a 3D-printed anemometer using SPIKE Prime to measure wind speed in real time, integrating STEM learning, engineering, and data collection.
items.text:
🌬️ What is wind — and how can we measure its strength with LEGO®?

In this lesson plan, students build a fully functional anemometer using LEGO® Education SPIKE™ Prime and a custom 3D-printed propeller. It’s a perfect way to combine STEM learning, engineering and real-world data collection — all in one engaging class. 📊💡

Here’s what the lesson includes:
1️⃣ A propeller designed from scratch in Autodesk Tinkercad,
2️⃣ Step-by-step building and programming instructions,
3️⃣ As a final result: a LEGO robot that measures wind speed in real time! 🌬️

🎯 To complete the lesson, you’ll need:
🔹 Autodesk Tinkercad
🔹 A 3D printer
🔹 LEGO® Education SPIKE™ Prime set

Looking for new ways to bring innovation into your classroom? This one is definitely worth a try! 🚀

#3DPrintingInEducation #LEGOEducation #SPIKEPrime #EducationalRobotics #STEMEducation #3DPrintYourRobot #MakerEducation #LessonPlanning #InnovativeTeaching #PythonForKids
